title: Russian Drone-Missile Strike on Odesa Kills Elderly Couple, Hits Merchant Ship
detail: Russia launched a mass overnight attack on Odesa, firing 107 Shahed-type loitering drones and 2 ballistic missiles. Ukrainian air defenses destroyed or jammed 96 drones, but 10 drones and both ballistic missiles struck targets. A 75-year-old married couple was killed in a residential building hit, and at least 15 people were injured (8 hospitalized). Three residential buildings caught fire and 36 residents including 2 children were evacuated. A Saint Kitts and Nevis-flagged bulk carrier was struck in the Black Sea shipping corridor near Odesa; all 22 crew survived. The attack follows Russia's sustained campaign of mass drone barrages against Ukrainian port cities.

media.caption: Aftermath of Russian drone and missile strike on residential buildings in Odesa, killing an elderly couple

title: EU Adopts 20th Russia Sanctions Package and €90B Ukraine Loan; Zelensky Demands Full Membership at Cyprus Summit
detail: The EU formally adopted its 20th package of sanctions against Russia on April 23: 120 new individual/entity listings, 20 Russian banks hit with transaction bans, 46 more shadow fleet tankers sanctioned (bringing total to 632), and a sector-wide ban on Russian crypto platforms. The EU also approved a €90 billion interest-free loan to Ukraine — covering two-thirds of Ukraine's needs for the next two years — after Hungary and Slovakia dropped their vetoes. Separately, President Zelensky attended the informal EU summit in Ayia Napa, Cyprus — the first EU summit in 16 years held without Hungary's Viktor Orban (who lost national elections). Zelensky rejected 'symbolic' EU membership, demanding full accession with a 'clear start date,' with a goal of reaching agreement by end-2026. Estonian PM Michal stated: 'When, not if.'

media.caption: Zelensky at the informal EU summit in Ayia Napa, Cyprus, demanding full EU membership with a clear start date
